acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_MORNING_CHRONOLOGY_CONTRADICTION] Pass if a cold reader can read the revised morning movement anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:107, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 without having to invent an off-page extra call or a missing ninety-minute gap: Tommy is still legible as the initial dispatcher caller, and Mallick's arrival no longer reads as occurring within roughly thirty to forty minutes of the deputies' arrival. Fail if Paul still appears to make the primary police call or if the detective still seems to arrive almost immediately after the deputies.

[DIALOGUE_MALLICK_CALIBRATION_ECHO] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:159 still contains the three required beats but no longer hinges on the current 'my mother always said you don't show up empty-handed' formulation or the current Kessner-to-Kessler apology cadence. Fail if Mallick still sounds like a lightly paraphrased style-bible demo rather than a fresh scene-specific speaker.

[PROSE_SPORTS_METAPHOR_AUTOPILOT] Pass if at least three of the cited beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171 are rewritten without football, game, season, sideline, or timeout framing, while Tommy's spoken cadence still reads like a coach. Fail if most of those moments still explain the same panic via repeated sports analogies.

[PROSE_PRESTIGE_INFLATION_CAPSTONES] Pass if the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:189 communicate Tommy's panic through concrete physical or environmental facts rather than essay-like summary phrases. Fail if those beats still end in abstract interpretive language that asks the reader to admire the sentence instead of track the immediate scene.

[PROSE_FORMULA_CH03] Pass if chapter 03 contains at most one 'the specific [noun]' construction and the remaining instances have been replaced with varied phrasing. Fail if two or more instances remain.

[COMEDY_REG_CH03] Pass if Tommy's Viagra chapter includes crude sexual humor in his internal monologue that reflects the locker-room context of the original joke, and his profanity density matches his style-bible profile. Fail if the chapter still treats the Viagra exclusively as a source of anxious guilt without the crude-comedy dimension.
[RED-057] Chapter 3 contains no more than 8 instances of 'something.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can read the revised morning movement anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:107, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 without having to invent an off-page extra call or a missing ninety-minute gap: Tommy is still legible as the initial dispatcher caller, and Mallick's arrival no longer reads as occurring within roughly thirty to forty minutes of the deputies' arrival. Fail if Paul still appears to make the primary police call or if the detective still seems to arrive almost immediately after the deputies.
findings.problem: The chapter's Saturday-morning sequence conflicts with the provided canon in two linked ways: line 97 makes Paul the police caller even though the boundary excerpt already established Tommy as the one talking to the dispatcher, and lines 97-149 compress Mallick's arrival to less than an hour after the deputies reach the house even though the continuity sheet preserves a deputy-only window before her later entrance. As written, the reader has to invent an unseen extra call and a missing stretch of time to reconcile the chapter with the allowed context packs.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:107, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 so the morning chronology matches canon. Either make Paul's study call an explicit follow-up that does not replace Tommy's already-established dispatcher call, or remove/reframe the exact timing language so Mallick's entrance no longer reads as happening almost immediately after the 8:30 deputy beat.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:159 still contains the three required beats but no longer hinges on the current 'my mother always said you don't show up empty-handed' formulation or the current Kessner-to-Kessler apology cadence. Fail if Mallick still sounds like a lightly paraphrased style-bible demo rather than a fresh scene-specific speaker.
findings.problem: Mallick's entrance dialogue echoes the style-bible calibration lines too closely. The wrong-name apology, the casserole explanation tied to her mother's empty-handed rule, and the rambling powder-room request arrive with nearly the same phrasing and sequencing as the non-copyable examples, so the scene reads less like live speech than like voice notes pasted into the manuscript.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Detective Mallick's entrance at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:159-167 to preserve the required factual beats (wrong name, casserole, bathroom request) while giving her new sentence shapes, new tangents, and a fresh self-correction pattern specific to this moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least three of the cited beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171 are rewritten without football, game, season, sideline, or timeout framing, while Tommy's spoken cadence still reads like a coach. Fail if most of those moments still explain the same panic via repeated sports analogies.
findings.problem: Tommy's POV keeps translating pressure through the same coaching-game scaffold until it starts to feel automatic rather than load-bearing. One or two sports conversions would be character-true; six major ones in a chapter this short flatten the panic into a repeated template and crowd out fresher textures of bodily shame, house geography, and evidence fear.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim or replace several of the sports-metaphor clusters at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175. Keep the failed halftime-talk beat and at most one later callback; render the remaining pressure beats through concrete bodily or situational detail already present in the scene.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:189 communicate Tommy's panic through concrete physical or environmental facts rather than essay-like summary phrases. Fail if those beats still end in abstract interpretive language that asks the reader to admire the sentence instead of track the immediate scene.
findings.problem: Several pressure beats slide out of Tommy's blunt close-third into polished abstract capstones that sound more admired than lived: the heart 'felt structural rather than rhythmic,' the bathroom light makes the room seem 'remembered rather than seen,' the bathroom trap becomes 'the specific helplessness' of a role, and the final light is 'very bright and very specific' as Tommy 'received it.' Those lines pull the chapter toward prestige-literary solemnity exactly where the style bible asks for muscular dark-comic immediacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the interpretive capstones at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:189 so the scene stays in Tommy's concrete sensory and bodily register. Keep the same dramatic beats, but let the image or action carry the pressure instead of abstractly naming what the reader should feel.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 03 contains at most one 'the specific [noun]' construction and the remaining instances have been replaced with varied phrasing. Fail if two or more instances remain.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:785 'the specific golden wood of a lake house'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:801 'the specific thing he could not afford'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:841 'the specific helplessness of a man whose job was to control outcomes'

evidence.excerpt: 785: He walked down the hallway toward the half bath near the front door. The hallway was fifteen feet of pine floor and pine walls, the specific golden wood of a lake house that had been absorbing summer after summer since the seventies, and Tommy walked it with his right hand in his jacket pocket and his fingers wrapped around the blister pack. He could feel the foil under his thumb, the smooth bumps of the three remaining pills.

801: He pushed himself up. His knees popped — the left one first, then the right, the specific sound of a body that had spent too many years on turf and was starting to complain about it. He looked at the floor where the pill had rolled. No powder, no residue, no blue smudge announcing what had happened here. He checked the baseboard where the pill had rested, and it was clean. He looked down the hallway in both directions — empty. Nobody had seen him. The kitchen was around the corner and the deputies were on the deck and the pill was in his fist and the hallway was just a hallway.

841: He thought about the seventeen times in his coaching career when the other team had scored with no time left and the scoreboard changed and the outcome was sealed and there was nothing to do but stand on the sideline and watch your season end — the specific helplessness of a man whose job was to control outcomes standing in a place where the outcome was already decided.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 785: He walked down the hallway toward the half bath near the front door. The hallway was fifteen feet of pine floor and pine walls, the specific golden wood of a lake house that had been absorbing summer after summer since the seventies, and Tommy walked it with his right hand in his jacket pocket and his fingers wrapped around the blister pack. He could feel the foil under his thumb, the smooth bumps of the three remaining pills.

findings.problem: Three instances of 'the specific [noun]' in a single chapter (180 lines), creating a rhythmic tic in Tommy's interiority. The construction abstracts sensation into a formula rather than rendering it through concrete image.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:785, 801, 841. Keep at most one instance (841 is strongest — the helplessness construction earns the weight). Replace the other two with concrete sensory detail or remove the intensifier. At line 785, 'the specific golden wood' can become simply 'the golden wood' or a more particular image of the pine.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Tommy's Viagra chapter includes crude sexual humor in his internal monologue that reflects the locker-room context of the original joke, and his profanity density matches his style-bible profile. Fail if the chapter still treats the Viagra exclusively as a source of anxious guilt without the crude-comedy dimension.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:671-861 — Tommy's Viagra chapter. The Viagra subplot has inherent crude-comedy potential that the chapter renders primarily through Tommy's internal embarrassment and physical comedy (the flushing scene). Tommy's dialogue with himself and his thoughts about the Viagra are anxious rather than crude. His style bible says his profanity is 'connective tissue' — here it's occasional emphasis.

findings.problem: The Viagra chapter plays Tommy's embarrassment straight rather than allowing the crudeness of the situation to surface in his internal monologue and physical reactions. Tommy's thoughts about giving Martin Viagra as a joke should include the crude humor that generated the joke in the first place — the locker-room context, the specific sexual ribbing that would have accompanied the gift. The chapter treats the Viagra as a source of guilt rather than mining the comic-horror of middle-aged male sexuality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:700-780. Add Tommy's internal crude humor about the Viagra situation — the locker-room jokes that preceded giving it to Martin, the specific sexual context that makes the gift both funny and terrible. Tommy's interiority should include the profane, sexually explicit register of a man who coaches teenage boys and talks about sex the way coaches talk about sex — bluntly, crudely, without nuance. The embarrassment is still the engine; the crude internal monologue is the texture.

findings.evidence: 49 em-dash-containing prose lines / 109 total prose lines = 44.9%.

findings.problem: Ch 3 He-opener density is 15.7%. Tommy's POV chapter begins every 6th sentence with 'He.'
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary sentence openings. Reduce He-openers to below 7%.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 3 contains no more than 8 instances of 'something.'
findings.evidence: 42 occurrences of 'something' / 6,963 prose words = 1 per 165 words. Manuscript-wide: 343/99,264 = 1 per 289 (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: Ch 3 has the highest 'something' density at 1 per 165 words — nearly 5x the threshold. Tommy's POV uses 'something' as a hedging filler in nearly every paragraph.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace or cut at least 30 of the 42 'something' instances in Ch 3. In most cases, name the thing specifically or cut the hedge entirely. 'Something about X' should become 'X.'

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
